title: glaucium corniculatum var. rubrum
intro: Glaucium corniculatum var. rubrum, also known as the red hornpoppy or red gromwell, is a variety of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the uterus and promote menstrual flow, making it a traditional treatment for menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. The red hornpoppy has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its sedative and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be consumed as a tea or tincture, or used topically as an ointment or cream, although its use should be cautioned against due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
